definitions.text: The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events.
examples: Time stops for nobody. the ebb and flow of time
Time flies when you're having fun.

definitions.text: The feeling of the passage of events and their relative duration, as experienced by an individual.
examples: Time flies when you're having fun.

definitions.text: To measure or record the time, duration, or rate of something.
examples: I used a stopwatch to time myself running around the block.

definitions.text: To choose when something commences or its duration.
examples: The President timed his speech badly, coinciding with the Super Bowl.
The bomb was timed to explode at 9:20 p.m.

definitions.text: To keep or beat time; to proceed or move in time.
Reminder by the umpire for the players to continue playing after their pause.
The umpire's call in prizefights, etc.
A call by a bartender to warn patrons that the establishment is closing and no more drinks will be served.
